newIncidents.title: DOJ moves to erase Bannon conviction for defying Jan. 6 congressional subpoena
newIncidents.description: Justice Department seeks dismissal of Steve Bannon's criminal contempt conviction for refusing to comply with House Jan. 6 committee subpoena, undermining congressional oversight authority and accountability for defying legislative investigations. Source

newIncidents.title: Trump directs intelligence agencies to support 'Stop the Steal' lawyer's legal efforts
newIncidents.description: President instructs spy agencies to provide intelligence to attorney involved in challenging 2020 election results, representing unprecedented politicization of intelligence community for partisan legal purposes. Source

newIncidents.title: DNI office warns whistleblower attorney against sharing classified complaint with Congress
newIncidents.description: Tulsi Gabbard's office attempts to prevent whistleblower's attorney from sharing classified complaint with congressional oversight committees, directly undermining statutory whistleblower protections and legislative oversight authority. Source

newIncidents.title: Trump administration moves to indict six Democratic lawmakers for protected speech
newIncidents.description: Justice Department attempts to prosecute opposition members of Congress for statements made in official capacity, prompting legal action from targeted lawmakers and representing unprecedented weaponization against political opponents. Source

newIncidents.title: White House immediately fires U.S. attorney selected by judges to replace Trump appointee
newIncidents.description: Administration removes U.S. attorney within hours of judicial appointment panel selecting him, creating direct conflict between executive and judicial branches over prosecutorial independence. Source

newIncidents.title: Trump vows to impose voter ID requirements 'whether approved by Congress or not'
newIncidents.description: President explicitly states intent to unilaterally implement federal election requirements without congressional authorization, directly challenging separation of powers and statutory authority over elections. Source

updates.summary: Multiple critical developments show accelerating institutional breakdown: DOJ moves to erase Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction, Trump directs intelligence agencies to support partisan election lawyers, administration attempts to indict six Democratic lawmakers, and White House fires judicially-appointed U.S. attorney within hours. Trump explicitly threatens to impose election requirements without congressional authorization, while DNI office attempts to block whistleblower complaints from reaching Congress.
